name: Braised Lamb Shanks
recipeIngredient: Salt And Freshly Ground Black Pepper
5 Milliliters To 10 Arrowroot, Mixed With A Little Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat half of the olive oil in a heavy flame-proof casserole.
2. Add the onion and sauté for 3 to 4 minutes until soft.
3. Add the garlic and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
4. Add the tomato puree, carrots, chopped tomatoes, red wine, lamb stock, herbs (rosemary and thyme), allspice, salt, and black pepper.
5. Bring the mixture to a boil.
6. Heat the remaining olive oil in a heavy-based frying pan.
7. Brown the lamb shanks on all sides.
8. Add the browned lamb shanks to the casserole and cover.
9. Cook in a preheated oven at 180°C (350°F) for 1½ to 2 hours, stirring occasionally until the meat comes away from the bone easily.
10. Lower the oven temperature to 110°C (225°F).
11. Remove the lamb shanks from the casserole and place them in an oven-safe dish; keep warm in the oven.
12. Strain the sauce into a saucepan and simmer until reduced by about half.
13. Add arrowroot mixed with water to thicken the sauce slightly if needed, then heat until thickened.
14. Place the lamb on a warmed serving plate and pour over the sauce.
15. Garnish with thyme and serve.
